page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.gn5i family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs, running on the KVM hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. The instance does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

From a resource density perspective, this instance represents an entry-level GPU tier. The absence of local storage and the moderate 2 Gbps network bandwidth are key tradeoffs to consider for data-intensive applications. It is qualitatively positioned as a budget-friendly option for workloads requiring hardware acceleration without the high cost of modern enterprise GPUs. Suitable workloads include light machine learning inference, video transcoding, and GPU-assisted rendering.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance from the ecs.gn5i family, running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. Hardware acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. The instance does not feature local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated computing, balancing moderate CPU and memory resources with dedicated graphics hardware. It is designed for workloads such as light machine learning inference, video transcoding, and GPU-assisted rendering where high-end enterprise GPU resources are not required.

tagline: An entry-level GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated x86_64 processing cores and an NVIDIA P4 graphics processor for light compute workloads.
